chunks.text: Fall 2023 Colloquium - EECS at Berkeley Fall 2023 Colloquium Fall 2023 Dedicated to hosting presentations at the intersection of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science, the EECS Colloquium offers a mix of short talks by faculty describing their latest research results as well as internationally distinguished speakers. It provides an opportunity for an exchange of ideas between our department’s community and the outside world. Wednesdays 4:00-5:00 PM, HP Auditorium, 306 Soda Hall (unless otherwise noted) Light refreshments served at 3:30 pm Subscribe to the eecs-announce mailing list through the web interface or via email so you can be apprised of upcoming talks. Eli Yablonovitch, UC Berkeley Carbon Negative Technology to Solve the Climate Crisis Youtube Webinar August 30, 2023 No talk this week September 6, 2023 Jeffrey Welser IBM Research: Inventing What’s Next Youtube Webinar September 13, 2023 No talk this week September 20, 2023 TBD September 27, 2023 Larry Zitnick, Meta-FAIR Modeling Atoms to Address Our Climate Crisis Youtube Webinar October 4, 2023 Jack Gallant, UC Berkeley Toward reverse engineering the deep networks of the human brain Youtube Webinar October 11, 2023 No talk this week October 18, 2023 CS Education Panel The Evolution of CS Education: Embracing Diversity, AI, and Competency Amidst Historical Context Youtube Webinar October 25, 2023 Edward Chang & Gopala Anumanchipalli Turning Brain Waves into Words Youtube Webinar November 1, 2023 Alison Gopnik Transmission vs. Truth, Imitation vs. Innovation: How children’s learning can inform AI Youtube Webinar November 8, 2023 No talk this week November
